name: Stitch Data Integration API
what_it_does: Stitch (Talend) cloud data integration REST API for ELT pipelines from 130+ sources to cloud data warehouses. Enables AI agents to manage data source connection and replication configuration for automated data pipeline setup, handle extraction job scheduling and monitoring for data pipeline operations, access replication progress and row count reporting for pipeline visibility, retrieve connection health and error monitoring for pipeline reliability management, manage destination configuration (Snowflake, Redshift, BigQuery, Databricks) for data warehouse automation, handle incremental replication key configuration for efficient data extraction, access schema discovery and field selection for selective data extraction, retrieve extraction logs and debugging data for pipeline troubleshooting automation, manage API source configuration for custom data source integration, and integrate Stitch with data catalog, dbt, and analytics platforms for modern data stack automation.
use_cases: Configuring data pipelines from data engineering agents
Monitoring pipeline health from DataOps agents
Managing data warehouse loading from analytics engineering agents
Troubleshooting extraction from data reliability agents
not_for: Real-time streaming data pipelines requiring sub-second latency (use Kafka or Kinesis)
Complex data transformation (use dbt or Spark)
Enterprise data governance without Talend ecosystem
best_when: A data engineering team, analytics organization, or data platform team wanting AI agents to automate Stitch pipeline configuration, monitor replication health, manage data warehouse loading, and troubleshoot extraction errors across 130+ cloud data sources.
avoid_when: STITCH/TALEND PRODUCT SUNSET RISK: Stitch was acquired by Talend (2018); Talend was acquired by Qlik (2023); verify current Stitch product roadmap under Qlik ownership; product investment and feature development may have changed under ownership transitions. PII DATA IN EXTRACTION PIPELINES: Automated Stitch extraction of tables containing PII must implement column-level field selection to exclude sensitive fields; automated full-table extraction without PII filtering copies raw PII to data warehouse and creates GDPR/CCPA data minimization violation. Singer tap compatibility — Stitch uses Singer open-source taps for many source connections; automated pipeline creation for Singer-based sources must validate tap version compatibility and maintenance status; unmaintained Singer taps may break silently with source API changes. Historical replication costs — automated historical table extraction for large source tables creates significant data warehouse storage and compute costs; implement row count and size estimation before triggering historical replication jobs.

auth.notes: Stitch uses API key authentication. REST API with JSON. Seattle, Washington HQ (Stitch by Talend/Qlik). Stitch founded 2016 as RJMetrics offshoot; Talend acquired 2018; Qlik acquired Talend 2023. 130+ data source connectors. Singer protocol open-source. Cloud data warehouse focus (Snowflake, Redshift, BigQuery). Competes with Fivetran and Airbyte for cloud ELT.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier with limited row volume; paid plans for higher row counts
pricing.paid_tiers: Monthly subscription based on row volume; enterprise contracts available
pricing.notes: Seattle WA. Qlik/Talend (acquired 2018/2023). Founded 2016. 130+ connectors. Singer protocol. Row-based pricing. Cloud data warehouse focus.

performance.rate_limits: API management rate limits per API key; extraction throughput depends on source API rate limits; documented in Stitch docs

known_agent_gotchas: QLIK/TALEND ACQUISITION PRODUCT UNCERTAINTY: Stitch has changed ownership twice (RJMetrics → Talend → Qlik); verify current product investment, roadmap, and connector maintenance status under Qlik ownership before committing to Stitch for long-term pipeline automation; some connectors may receive reduced maintenance

PII column field selection mandatory for GDPR/CCPA compliance — automated Stitch pipeline creation must explicitly configure field selection to exclude PII columns (SSN, DOB, financial data, health data); default full-table replication without field selection copies raw PII to data warehouse and creates GDPR data minimization violation

No webhooks — extraction job completion, error, and pipeline state change events require polling Stitch API; no push notification for pipeline events; implement polling with exponential backoff for extraction job monitoring

Singer tap version pinning — Singer-based source connectors may receive breaking updates; automated pipeline creation should pin Singer tap version and implement update testing workflow before version upgrades

Historical replication row cost — automated historical table full replication for large source tables (>100M rows) creates significant row usage cost against Stitch billing plan; implement table size check and cost estimation before triggering historical replication

Source credential rotation — automated credential rotation for Stitch source connections (database passwords, API keys) must update Stitch connection credential in lockstep; automated source credential rotation without Stitch credential update causes immediate pipeline failure
agent_readiness.security_notes: ELT data integration. SOC2, GDPR. API key. US/EU. Source system credentials and data pipeline configuration.

security_score_components.security_notes: Data integration. SOC2, GDPR. API key. US/EU. Source system credentials and pipeline data.
